title: bauhinia dipetala
intro: Bauhinia dipetala, also known as the orange hill or pink orchid tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The extracts of Bauhinia dipetala contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as supporting immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, Bauhinia dipetala is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts may also be used in capsule or powder form for convenient consumption.
